scope: Viewport surface reconstruction only. The Wave/Bump layer appearance changes rendered normals but not mesh vertices; it is not a slicer or G-code toolpath.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity are renderer-specific units. Blender's Medium High Contrast look clips this neutral filament almost entirely white. The browser keeps the authored Wave/Bump graph and uses an asset-scoped ambient contribution to reproduce that exposure ceiling without changing the mesh or shared material implementation.

interpretation: The authored geometry, material assignment, col field, and shared filament graph are recovered, and the aligned silhouette is near-identical. Blender's contrast transform clips 99.98% of the surface pixels white. Matching that capture-specific exposure ceiling removes the former dark subpixel bands: RGB MAE is 0.00070 and the black/white threshold disagreement is zero. The viewport material remains separate from mesh and G-code geometry.
